title: Traveling Harriet Tubman statue stops in Syracuse for a week as part of 750-mile walk
summary: A traveling 9-foot Harriet Tubman statue depicting her work on the Underground Railroad has stopped at Syracuse City Hall plaza for a week as part of a 750-mile journey. This cultural and historical initiative highlights civic engagement around equity and community heritage.

political_signal.description: Public display of historical equity narrative at municipal center suggests alignment with values around historic preservation and community narrative celebrating Civil Rights history.
